title: Irish Beef and Citrus Dumplings
description: A hearty and flavorful take on traditional Irish beef stew with a twist of citrus. This comforting dish is perfect for a cold winter's night.
instructions: 1. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook the dumplings according to package instructions or until they float to the surface.
2. In a large Dutch oven, cook the bacon over medium heat until crispy. Remove the bacon and set aside.
3. Add the sliced onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
4. Add the beef to the pot and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
5. Add the carrots and potatoes to the pot, along with the beef broth and thyme. Bring to a boil, then cover and transfer to the oven.
6. Bake the stew at 300°F (150°C) for 2-3 hours, or until the beef is tender.
7. Stir in the flour to thicken the stew, if desired.
8. Serve the stew hot, topped with the cooked dumplings and crispy bacon.
9. Garnish with fresh thyme, if desired.
